steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS W/O CONTRAST)

discordance.evidence: OSH: 'fever was 103.5 and labs were notable for lactate of 3.9, white count of 14.8 with 90% neutrophils' versus current admission exam showing 'Soft, mildly distended. Normal bowel sound. Mild tenderness in LLQ. No guarding or rebound tenderness. Negative ___ sign. Negative psoas sign' and current lactate 1.5 — but also current Troponin T: 0.13 ng/mL (ref 0.0-0.01) and Creatinine: 3.6 mg/dL (ref 0.5-1.2) with eGFR 17-20, Anion Gap: 19.0 mEq/L, in a patient with only mild LLQ tenderness and benign abdominal exam

alarm_detect.summary: A markedly elevated troponin (0.13 ng/mL, 13× upper limit of normal) combined with acute-on-chronic kidney injury (creatinine 3.6, eGFR 17-20) and an elevated anion gap (19) are not explained by the leading diagnosis of LLQ pain/constipation/post-surgical infection, representing a striking discordance that warrants urgent further evaluation before defaulting to routine abdominal workup.

alarm_resolve.reason: The CT abdomen/pelvis without contrast is well-reasoned for the abdominal source of infection and appropriately accounts for the renal impairment, but the reasoning makes no mention of the markedly elevated troponin (13× ULN) — the most pressing discordant finding — leaving a potential acute cardiac or ischemic process entirely unaddressed.
